perturbation.original_text: “Information” means any and all confidential information or data exchanged, submitted or otherwise disclosed in respect of or further to the Purpose or prepared for or in relation to the Purpose, including but not limited to written Submission documentation, due diligence materials, contractual documentation, reports, and the fact that the Parties have entered into this Agreement and are discussing and considering a business relationship;
perturbation.changed_text: “Information” means any and all confidential information or data exchanged, submitted or otherwise disclosed in respect of or further to the Purpose or prepared for or in relation to the Purpose, excluding preliminary discussions and ideas that have not been documented in writing, including but not limited to written Submission documentation, due diligence materials, contractual documentation, reports, and the fact that the Parties have entered into this Agreement and are discussing and considering a business relationship;
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Information' is narrowed to exclude 'preliminary discussions and ideas that have not been documented in writing.' However, clause 2 requires the Receiving Party to 'hold the Information in confidence.' This alteration creates a contradiction: if 'Information' now excludes undocumented ideas, are those ideas still protected by the confidentiality clause in clause 2? This introduces an ambiguity directly into clause 2a.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication o r otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of t he Information (or any further information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreeme nt; and d. at the request and direction of the Disclosing Party, and without delay, return or destroy any Information provided to it pursuant to this Agreement and any copies of such Information, except that one copy may be kept by the Receiving Party for arc hival purposes and for the purpose of defending itself against any claims arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Information' to exclude undocumented ideas. However, the contradicted text states the 'Receiving Party shall hold the Information in confidence'. Since the changed text excludes undocumented ideas from being considered Information, it creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether those ideas are still protected by the confidentiality clause.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication o r otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose;
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence, unless the Receiving Party deems the information to be irrelevant to the Project's success, and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication o r otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose;
perturbation.explanation: Clause 2(a) is altered to allow the Receiving Party to disclose information deemed 'irrelevant to the Project's success'. This directly contradicts the initial statement requiring them to 'hold the Information in confidence'. The ambiguity arises as it is not defined anywhere in the agreement, whom decides the Information to be irrelevant to the Project's success. This introduces subjective criteria to a previously objective requirement to keep information confidential, thus creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall with regard to any Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party on or after the Effective Date: a. hold the Information in confidence, unless the Receiving Party deems the information to be irrelevant to the Project's success, and, except as is otherwise stated herein or agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party, shall not disclose or make available the Information by publication o r otherwise to any third party (including for the avoidance of doubt, disclosure in any patent application or to any patent office) and shall use any Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement only for carrying out the Purpose; b. make copies of t he Information (or any further information derived from the Information) in whatever form or medium only to the extent that the copies are reasonably necessary for the Purpose and clearly mark all such copies as confidential; c. take all necessary and proper security precautions (and at least as great as those it takes to safeguard its own information) to safeguard every part of the Information to prevent it from being disclosed or otherwise made available to any third party except as permitted by this Agreeme nt; and d. at the request and direction of the Disclosing Party, and without delay, return or destroy any Information provided to it pursuant to this Agreement and any copies of such Information, except that one copy may be kept by the Receiving Party for arc hival purposes and for the purpose of defending itself against any claims arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation, allowing the Receiving Party to disclose information deemed 'irrelevant to the Project's success'. This directly contradicts the original requirement to 'hold the Information in confidence' and creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which restates the changed text and makes the entire Confidentiality Clause inherently contradictory.
